sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: West Ham United are in poor form, winless in their last 9 matches, with 4 losses and a draw in their last 5. Nottingham Forest are also struggling, having lost their last 4 matches, including heavy defeats to Aston Villa and Everton. Both teams are in desperate need of points to climb out of the relegation zone.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two head-to-head encounters, West Ham dominated the most recent with a 3-0 away win, while Nottingham Forest edged out a 2-1 victory in the earlier meeting. Historically, matches between these teams have been high-scoring, with over 2.5 goals in 5 of their last 6 encounters.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: West Ham (18th, 14 points) have struggled defensively, conceding 41 goals this season, the highest in the league. Nottingham Forest (17th, 18 points) have scored fewer goals (19) but have a slightly better defensive record. Both teams rank poorly in key areas like big chances created and possession.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For West Ham, Jarrod Bowen (6 goals) and Lucas Paquetá (4 goals) are vital in attack, while Callum Hudson-Odoi (3 goals, 7.38 avg. rating vs West Ham) is a standout for Forest. Their performances could decide the outcome of this tight contest.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: West Ham’s high conceding rate and inability to keep clean sheets (17 matches without one) suggest vulnerabilities at the back. Forest’s tendency to concede first (4 of last 5) could give West Ham an edge if they capitalize early, but Forest’s late scoring ability (36% of goals after 76 mins) is a threat.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Sofascore’s probabilities (home win 34%, draw 28%, away win 38%) and recent form, Nottingham Forest hold a slight edge due to West Ham’s prolonged slump. However, playing at the London Stadium could inspire West Ham to end their winless streak. Expect a close, high-scoring game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, West Ham United lead Nottingham Forest 1-0 at the London Stadium. The solitary goal came early in the match from an own goal by Murillo, giving the home side the edge. Despite the scoreline, the game has been closely contested with both teams showing intent, though West Ham have been more clinical in their approach. The Hammers have managed to capitalize on a defensive error, while Nottingham Forest have struggled to convert their possession into meaningful chances.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: West Ham United have shown a balanced performance in the first half, with a decent share of possession and a strong presence in the final third. Their defensive structure has been solid, limiting Forest's clear opportunities. Nottingham Forest, on the other hand, have dominated possession for large periods but have failed to create big chances, with their expected goals reflecting a lack of cutting edge in attack. Their defensive lapse early on has cost them, and they’ve been less effective in winning duels compared to the home side.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum review
halftimeSections.text: The first half began with Nottingham Forest showing early attacking intent, pushing forward in the opening minutes. However, West Ham quickly responded with a strong spell of their own, gaining momentum and eventually taking the lead. After conceding, Forest regained control with a sustained period of pressure, looking to equalize. Towards the middle of the half, West Ham enjoyed another dominant phase, consistently threatening in the attacking third. As the half drew to a close, Forest once again ramped up their efforts with significant attacking spells, but they couldn’t find a breakthrough.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players at half-time
halftimeSections.text: For West Ham, Valentín Castellanos has been a focal point in attack, showing promise in creating opportunities despite missing a big chance. Crysencio Summerville has also contributed with shots on target and key passes, adding to the Hammers’ threat. On the defensive end, Oliver Scarles has stood out with crucial clearances and tackles. For Nottingham Forest, Murillo’s own goal has overshadowed an otherwise decent defensive display, while Neco Williams has been active with shots on target. Callum Hudson-Odoi has shown flashes of creativity but needs more support to make a real impact.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, West Ham United will likely aim to maintain their defensive solidity while seeking to exploit counter-attacking opportunities to extend their lead. Nottingham Forest, trailing by a goal, are expected to push harder for an equalizer, potentially taking more risks in attack. Their higher possession in the first half suggests they can control the game if they sharpen their finishing. However, West Ham’s home advantage and current momentum could see them hold firm or even add to their tally if Forest overcommit.
